Feature set: Center/unmasked expression only; no neighbor tokens.
Target or metric: Normalized target expression or task proxy.
Fairness role: Defines what center expression already explains before neighbor context receives credit.
Known risk: If the baseline is underfit, context can appear useful; if it is too flexible, it can absorb true neighbor effects.

Feature set: Evidence-routed context use; does not spend real context unless strict gates pass.
Fairness role: Measures whether routed context explains residual target signal beyond expression-only evidence.
Known risk: A conservative router may abstain from weak real effects that a future stronger model could exploit.

Feature set: Center expression plus true-neighbor context for every row.
Fairness role: Tests whether unconditional context use creates gains or false context when compared with expression-only.
Known risk: Can overuse spatial context and convert confounded/negative rows into false-context claims.

Feature set: Center expression plus random, coordinate-shuffled, or distant neighbor context.
Fairness role: Separates physical-neighbor evidence from generic spatial, batch, or sampling artifacts.
Known risk: If controls are unavailable or mismatched, residual gains may be artifact-sensitive.

Fairness role: Prevents residual hints from being promoted into biological context-benefit claims without full gate support.
Known risk: This is a benchmark claim boundary, not proof that all biological residual context effects are absent.
